perturbation.original_text: 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with the Licensed Services and any business provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this Agreement, and except as provided above, neither the Licensee nor any of its affiliates, owners, directors, officers, employees or agents shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivatives without the prior express written consent of the Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with the Licensed Services and any business provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark freely and without restriction. the Licensee nor any of its affiliates, owners, directors, officers, employees or agents shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivatives without the prior express written consent of the Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. Originally, Section 1.1 grants a limited license, emphasizing the Licensee's use should be "only to the extent permitted under this Agreement." The modified text states the Licensee can use the Licensed Mark "freely and without restriction," which directly contradicts the subsequent clause requiring Licensor's consent for other uses and the entire premise of a defined scope of license. The contradiction creates uncertainty about the extent of permitted use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with the Licensed Services and any business provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark freely and without restriction. the Licensee nor any of its affiliates, owners, directors, officers, employees or agents shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivatives without the prior express written consent of the Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion. All rights not expressly granted to the Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor. Upon written notification by the Licensor Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 to the Licensee of noncompliance with the Licensor's quality standards in any material respect, such Licensee shall take appropriate steps, in a commercially reasonable time frame, not to exceed sixty (60) days, to cure such noncompliance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 1.1 introduces a direct contradiction. The original text grants a limited license for using the Licensed Mark, emphasizing use 'only to the extent permitted under this Agreement.' The changed text states the Licensee can use the Licensed Mark 'freely and without restriction,' contradicting the subsequent clause that requires the Licensor's consent for other uses. The contradicted text, being the entire changed Section 1.1, reiterates this contradiction. This conflict makes it unclear whether the Licensee is bound by any restrictions at all, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1. Term. The license granted to the Licensee under this Agreement shall continue perpetually. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable (a) by the Licensor (i) at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that the Licensor or the Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of the Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensor to the Licensee or (b) by the Licensee (i) at any time in the event such Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of the Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the Licensor or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensee to the Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1. Term. The license granted to the Licensee under this Agreement shall continue perpetually, with no exceptions. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable (a) by the Licensor (i) at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that the Licensor or the Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of the Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensor to the Licensee or (b) by the Licensee (i) at any time in the event such Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of the Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the Licensor or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensee to the Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation, 'with no exceptions,' contradicts the rest of the paragraph, which details several scenarios where the agreement *can* be terminated. By explicitly stating that the perpetual term has 'no exceptions,' the subsequent clauses outlining termination conditions create an in-text contradiction, creating uncertainty about the actual term of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1. Term. The license granted to the Licensee under this Agreement shall continue perpetually, with no exceptions. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable (a) by the Licensor (i) at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that the Licensor or the Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of the Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensor to the Licensee or (b) by the Licensee (i) at any time in the event such Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of the Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the Licensor or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensee to the Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The addition of "with no exceptions" directly contradicts the subsequent clauses within the same section (4.1) that outline specific conditions under which the agreement can be terminated. The original text acknowledges exceptions to the perpetual term, while the changed text explicitly denies any exceptions, creating a clear and irreconcilable in-text contradiction about the term's duration and termination possibilities.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2. Assignment. The Licensee shall not sublicense, assign, pledge or grant as security or otherwise encumber or transfer to any third party all or any part of its rights or duties under this Agreement, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent from the Licensor, which consent the Licensor may grant or withhold in its 3 Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 sole and absolute discretion. Any purported transfer or other encumbrance without such consent shall be void ab initio.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2. Assignment. The Licensee shall freely sublicense, assign, pledge or grant as security or otherwise encumber or transfer to any third party all or any part of its rights or duties under this Agreement, in whole or in part. without the prior written consent from the Licensor, which consent the Licensor may grant or withhold in its 3 Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 sole and absolute discretion. Any purported transfer or other encumbrance without such consent shall be void ab initio.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'freely' directly contradicts the subsequent requirement for prior written consent from the Licensor. The clause initially prohibits assignment without consent, giving the Licensor control. The added word suggests the Licensee has an unrestricted right to assign, only to have it restricted again. This creates immediate confusion about the Licensee's rights regarding assignment of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2. Assignment. The Licensee shall freely sublicense, assign, pledge or grant as security or otherwise encumber or transfer to any third party all or any part of its rights or duties under this Agreement, in whole or in part. without the prior written consent from the Licensor, which consent the Licensor may grant or withhold in its 3 Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 sole and absolute discretion. Any purported transfer or other encumbrance without such consent shall be void ab initio.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'freely' in the changed text directly contradicts the subsequent requirement for prior written consent from the Licensor. The clause initially prohibits assignment without consent, giving the Licensor control. The added word suggests the Licensee has an unrestricted right to assign, only to have it restricted again. This creates immediate confusion about the Licensee's rights regarding assignment of the agreement, and conflicts with the initial intention. This is a structural flaw that creates an in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Definitions.
